title: Order by disorder up to arbitrarily high temperature
abstract: We prove that a class of classical lattice models on $\mathbb{Z}^d$ ($d \geq 2$) with on-site space $\mathbb{N}_0$ exhibits long-range checkerboard order at sufficiently high temperature. The model has a nearest-neighbour interaction $f : \mathbb{N}_0 \times \mathbb{N}_0 \to [0,\infty)$ satisfying four structural conditions, subsuming the recently introduced power-law model of Han--Huang--Komargodski--Lucas--Popov (arXiv:2503.22789) as a special case. The ordering mechanism is purely entropic: the checkerboard configurations are not energy minimisers, but are selected by the partial trace over occupation numbers in the $β\to 0$ limit. The proof uses Pirogov--Sinai theory and the key input is a Peierls bound.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous mathematical proof that certain classical lattice models can exhibit long-range order at arbitrarily high temperatures. This 'order by disorder' phenomenon occurs because entropy, rather than energy minimization, selects specific checkerboard configurations in the high-temperature limit.
main_problem: Proving the existence of long-range checkerboard order in a class of classical lattice models at arbitrarily high temperatures via an entropic mechanism.
main_result: The author proves that a class of classical lattice models exhibits long-range checkerboard order in the high-temperature limit (beta -> 0), where the order is driven by entropy rather than energy minimization.
method: The proof utilizes Pirogov-Sinai theory, cluster expansion, and a Peierls-type bound on contour surface energy to demonstrate phase coexistence.
model_or_system: A classical lattice model on a hypercubic lattice Z^d (d >= 2) with unbounded occupation numbers (bosons) and a nearest-neighbor interaction function f(n_x, n_y) satisfying specific symmetry and growth conditions.
key_observables: Difference between the Gibbs measure of occupation labels and the reference checkerboard configurations; bulk free energy density.
important_parameters: Inverse temperature (beta -> 0 limit), dimension (d >= 2), and the growth exponent (alpha) of the level-set counting function.
main_assumptions: The interaction function must satisfy four structural conditions (symmetry, vanishing on empty sites, strict penalty on occupied pairs, and specific growth); the analysis assumes a large-volume limit.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates a configuration with two contours, showing the boundary and the patterned regions representing the boundaries of the contours.
paper_structure: The paper establishes the model and its interaction properties, develops a polymer representation via cluster decomposition, applies Pirogov-Sinai theory to bound contour weights, and concludes with a proof of phase coexistence and uniform convergence to the checkerboard state.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: A configuration with two contours γ1 and γ2 with the boundary Bpωq denoted by the dark grey region and the patterned regions representing boundaries of the contours. The outer boundary has the phase η1 and the interior boundary has the same phase as that of the interior components along which they are drawn.
